1.intent: Synthesize Zenoh-compatible networking stack from SCXML + SCE Forge as single source of truth; two interoperable backends.
outputs_bullets: AP backend: Rust, std, tokio runtime, Linux x86_64 / aarch64
MCU backend: C11, no_std-equivalent, no heap; SoC TBD until deploy authoring
Wire-interoperability with upstream zenohd for the sec 2 documented subset
caveats_bullets: Not a rewrite of zenoh/zenoh-pico; demonstration that SCE synthesizes industrial wire protocols.

10.intent: Per-binding QoS configuration sourced from deploy.yaml and emitted as runtime structs (AP) or static const tables (MCU).

10/10.1.intent: deploy.yaml QoS ‚ÄĒ per-binding priority/reliability/express/congestion_control declared in machine binding block; typed config consumed by codegen.

10/10.2.intent: QoS config codegen ‚ÄĒ AP emits Rust const QosConfig (data section); MCU emits C const struct in .rodata (Flash, no RAM cost).

11.intent: Multi-layer testing strategy: byte-equivalence parity, wire replay, upstream interop, no-alloc guard, drift detection, fuzz.

11/11.1.intent: Cross-backend parity gate ‚ÄĒ every algorithm/codec with sce:test-vector runs on both backends and is byte-diffed; gate on merge.

11/11.2.intent: Wire replay tests ‚ÄĒ pcap captures from upstream zenoh peers replayed into generated stack with declared expected event sequence.

11/11.3.intent: Upstream interop CI ‚ÄĒ dockerized zenohd + AP node + MCU host-mode build (lwIP shim, renode/QEMU later) asserting full handshake + pub/sub roundtrip.

11/11.4.intent: MCU heap prohibition enforced as five layers ‚ÄĒ direct trap, linker wrap, libc pin, call-graph reachability, exception ban ‚ÄĒ not runtime trap alone.
rationale_bullets: Layer 1 direct trap: malloc/calloc/realloc/free stubs emit __builtin_trap on call.
Layer 2 linker --wrap: -Wl,--wrap on alloc symbols routes libc-internal indirect calls to traps.
Layer 3 libc pin: newlib-nano or picolibc tiny stdio prevents allocating printf path.
Layer 4 call-graph: post-link analyzer rejects any reach from out/mcu to deny-listed allocator.
Layer 5 exception ban: -fno-exceptions/-fno-rtti/-fno-unwind-tables blocks libstdc++ machinery.

11/11.5.intent: Drift gate: every emitted file carries source-hash + template-hash; sce-build verify recomputes and fails on mismatch; manual edits to out/ forbidden.
rationale_bullets: Per-file header carries source-hash + template-hash (sha256 over inputs and sce-build binary).
sce-build verify recomputes both hashes and fails the gate on mismatch (CI + pre-commit).
Fixed artifact set: out/{ap,mcu}, Cargo.toml, CMakeLists.txt, linker fragments, sce_sourcemap.json.
Manual edits to out/ forbidden: path is RFC or sce:extern; exceptions tracked with expiry date.
sce_sourcemap.json hash mismatch surfaces as traceability/sourcemap-source-hash-mismatch.

11/11.6.intent: Adversarial input fuzz testing on generated codec/parsers; deferred Phase D+ work; complements ¬ß11.1 cross-backend parity.

12.intent: Generated AP/MCU code dependency surface: SCE crates, runtime crates, intrinsics, and user-provided platform pieces.

12/12.1.intent: Generated code dependency surface ‚ÄĒ AP Rust pulls sce_core/forge_runtime/link_runtime_tokio/intrinsics + bytes/tokio/heapless; MCU C pulls forge_runtime_c/link_runtime_lwip/intrinsics + lwIP/HAL.

12/12.2.intent: Runtime crate versioning ‚ÄĒ SemVer; generated code pins exact minor at build time via Cargo.toml/CMakeLists constraint to avoid SSoT drift between codegen and runtime.

12/12.3.intent: External user-provided surface ‚ÄĒ MCU consumer provides SoC HAL/lwIP config/master linker script; AP binary ships self-contained.

13.intent: Pre-SCE prose and tooling work: wire spec, pcap corpus, deploy skeletons, FSM sketches, meta-source generator (RFC 5.G workaround).

14.intent: Living-doc maintenance notes: when Phase A lands, what sections to revise and how to track architecture-vs-code drift.

15.intent: Placeholder for observability stack (logging, metrics, tracing); decisions deferred per section 14 living-doc notes.

16.intent: Placeholder for performance targets (OPEN handshake latency on MCU, AP TX throughput); awaits first benchmark numbers.

2.intent: Scope partition: MVP (zenoh-pico parity), Deferred (with migration path), Permanent constraints, Extensibility invariants, Protocol pinning.

2/2.0.intent: Long-term migration vision (every Zenoh feature eventually lands) + MVP criterion (full zenoh-pico parity, peer+client both backends).

2/2.1.intent: MVP scope table ‚ÄĒ peer+client modes, full message set, 5 transports, QoS dims, bounded KeyExpr matching, fragmentation, both targets, zero-copy posture.

2/2.2.intent: Phase D+ deferred items (router mode, BLE/Raweth, API shim, shared-mem, admin/scripting) each carries an explicit migration path; not permanently excluded.

2/2.3.intent: MCU-class permanent constraints (no router, no aggregation, no heap, no scripting, no unbounded KeyExpr, no dynamic loading); AP unaffected.

2/2.4.intent: Six extensibility invariants: static-first, link-extensible, kinds-additive, library-not-monolith, platform gating, out/ SSoT.
rationale_bullets: Static-first, dynamic-opt-in: every MCU-static feature should admit an AP-dynamic variant.
Link drivers extensible: open set via target plugin, not closed enum.
Kinds additive: ForgeKind set grows; existing kinds remain stable across versions.
Generated code exports as library, not monolith: out/ap and out/mcu are library targets.
Platform gating only when physically necessary; keep pool/link/worker kinds platform-neutral.
out/ is SSoT-downstream: manual edits forbidden; new features land via SCE extensions.

2/2.5.intent: Zenoh 1.x wire format pinned via deploy.yaml protocol block; additive extensions allowed within 1.x, 2.x is a separate scope decision.

3.intent: Four design invariants: zero-runtime abstraction, location transparency, strict SSoT, hardware-aware codegen.

3/3.1.intent: Zero-Runtime Abstraction ‚ÄĒ no vtables, no dynamic dispatch in the hot path; all bindings resolve at build time to direct calls (SCE AOT philosophy carried through).

3/3.2.intent: Location Transparency ‚ÄĒ SCXML uses logical identities; physical transport realization and addressing live in deploy.yaml; same SCXML deploys to AP or MCU.

3/3.3.intent: Strict SSoT ‚ÄĒ wire format / algorithms / FSMs defined once in SCXML+Forge; AP and MCU are descendants; cross-backend drift is a generator bug, not expected.

3/3.4.intent: Hardware-Aware Codegen ‚ÄĒ MCU code integrates with target SoC capabilities (SRAM placement, DMA pairing, alignment) declared via deploy.yaml; AP/MCU share no memory.

4.intent: Mapping of SCE existing capabilities, RFC-requested extensions, and watching-zenoh authoring scope.

4/4.1.intent: SCE existing surface ‚ÄĒ SCXML parser/IR/W3C algos, expression transpiler, 11 Forge kinds, multi-language codegen, no_std Rust runtime, diagnostics + composition.

4/4.2.intent: SCE extensions requested via RFC ‚ÄĒ new kinds (algorithm/link/buffer-pool/worker/bounded-collection), codec DSL, fragment pattern, C11 backend, no_std statechart, sce:extern, deploy fields.

4/4.3.intent: Project authoring scope ‚ÄĒ SCXML statecharts, Forge codec/algorithm/bounded-collection/link/pool/worker sources, deploy.yaml variants, runtime crates, optional API shim.

5.intent: Cargo workspace structure: out/ap library, crates/watching_zenoh_{api,bin}, plugin extension path.

6.intent: Build stages from SCXML+SCE through codegen, out/, cargo, to AP/MCU artifacts; per-target artifact list.

6/6.1.intent: Build pipeline stages ‚ÄĒ sce-codegen single-invocation parse/validate/IR/typecheck/const-fold/emit/sidecar driving Cargo + CMake builds.

6/6.2.intent: Per-target artifacts ‚ÄĒ AP gets src/Cargo.toml/build.rs; MCU gets headers/sources/CMake/linker fragment with linker-flavor matrix (GNU LD default, scatter/ICF/os-managed via target plugin).

7.intent: Runtime layout: AP backend (Rust+tokio), MCU backend (C11+lwIP), shared runtime crates/headers.

7/7.1.intent: AP backend stack ‚ÄĒ generated code on top of sce_core + sce_link_runtime_tokio + Rust std/bytes/tokio; tokio::spawn workers, mpsc channels, BytesMut pools.

7/7.2.intent: MCU stack ‚ÄĒ generated code over sce_forge_runtime_c + sce_link_runtime_lwip; cooperative tick scheduler with WCET budget; static pools + DMA descriptors.

7/7.3.intent: Shared runtime crates/headers ‚ÄĒ sce_core (AP only), sce_forge_runtime (Rust+C variants), sce_link_runtime_{tokio,lwip}, sce_intrinsics_runtime (whitelisted).

8.intent: Wire format layering: scouting/transport/network/zenoh/extensions; FSM, codec, link layers.

8/8.1.intent: Wire format stack layer diagram ‚ÄĒ Application -> Session FSM -> Network FSM -> Codec -> Link -> OS/HAL; bottom-up data flow on RX, top-down on TX.

8/8.2.intent: Session FSM sketch ‚ÄĒ Init/Scouting/Opening/Established/Closing path authored as session_unicast/multicast.scxml; full model lives in docs/session-fsm.md.

8/8.3.intent: Codec layer ‚ÄĒ one SCXML file per message type using RFC ¬ß5.B extensions (flags/vle/present-if/len-prefix/TLV); test vectors drive cross-backend parity.

8/8.4.intent: Link layer ‚ÄĒ kind=link SCXML files (UDP unicast/multicast/TCP/Serial/WS) with framer + RX-pool + backpressure declared; driver and address bound via deploy.yaml.

9.intent: Zero-copy: TX always zero-copy; RX happy path zero-copy; oversized/fragment stage-copy; pool placement; platform-aware link.

9/9.1.intent: TX path zero-copy ‚ÄĒ Codec encodes directly into pool slot, link send-from-slot, DMA reads via prebuilt descriptor; pool slot in DMA-coherent SRAM.

9/9.2.intent: RX happy path zero-copy ‚ÄĒ DMA writes pre-programmed RX slot; codec parses in place; event emitted with slot borrow; slot returned to freelist.

9/9.3.intent: Stage-copy RX path for oversized/multi-DMA ‚ÄĒ one copy DMA-slot to stage; build-time fragmentation analysis; stage_copy_policy warn/error/forbid per machine.

9/9.4.intent: Pool placement across .dtcm/.sram1/.flash regions; per-pool placement declared in kind source; memory regions in deploy.yaml; linker fragment ties them.

9/9.5.intent: Platform-aware link substrate: 5-row matrix (MCU bare_metal, linux+epoll, linux+io_uring, QNX, etc) sharing pool FSM.

architecture--watching-zenoh.intent: Pre-implementation design document for watching-zenoh (Zenoh stack synthesized from SCE), blocked on SCE Phase A.
caveats_bullets: Status: Pre-implementation (2026-04-24); blocked on SCE Phase A landing.
Becomes living reference updated in lockstep with code once SCE Phase A lands.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface.intent: Defines sce_intrinsics_runtime symbol surface (C11/Rust variants) for atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem.
inputs_bullets: RFC sec 5.I sce:extern whitelist ‚ÄĒ atomics, fences, cache maintenance, IRQ control
RFC sec 5.J.2 ‚ÄĒ statechart no_std HAL trait shape (ticks/wake/irq-save)
RFC sec 5.K target_plugin extension ‚ÄĒ per-SoC HMAC + HW semaphores out of core whitelist
session-fsm sec 2.7 G-SFM-5 ‚ÄĒ HMAC + RNG primitive ownership question (OQ-W15)
reassembly-fsm sec 6 ‚ÄĒ cache maintenance pinning (sec 5.E pool FSM call sites)
ARCHITECTURE sec 9.5 5-row matrix ‚ÄĒ same symbol set, different bodies per row
outputs_bullets: Categorized symbol table marking core whitelist vs target plugin per category
sec 5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait mapped onto these symbols
Cross-reference into RFC sec 5.I diagnostic list for authoring violation traceability
caveats_bullets: OQ-W14 (HW-sem standardization) and OQ-W15 (HMAC+RNG ownership) gate final shape.
Initial proposal: option 2 for HMAC (target plugin), option 1 for RNG (core whitelist).
Non-outputs: symbol body implementations, per-SoC HMAC accelerator binding.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-1-a.intent: Atomics core whitelist ‚ÄĒ per-width (u8-u64+usize) load/store/cas/fetch_* with explicit memory ordering encoded in the symbol name; no integer ordering parameter.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-2-f.intent: Fences core whitelist ‚ÄĒ atomic_fence_{acquire/release/acq_rel/seq_cst}, compiler_barrier (no runtime cost), dma_fence (DSB on ARMv7-M+, no-op on linux).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-3-c.intent: Cache maintenance core whitelist on MCU, no-op on AP linux (kernel-coherent); emitted only on ¬ß5.E pool lifecycle FSM edges; direct <sce:call> rejected.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-4-i.intent: IRQ control core whitelist (optional) ‚ÄĒ irq_save/irq_restore; only emitted for workers with sce:critical-section (RFC ¬ß5.D, Phase C+); MVP deploys do not use these.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-5-r.intent: RNG target plugin row (OQ-W15 (a) Q1 ratified Round 11: core whitelist rejected; sce_random_fill consumed by stateless_accept cookie HMAC key gen).
caveats_bullets: Q1 = No ratified Round 11: SCE BASELINE_SYMBOLS boundary is architecture-fixed primitives only.
Impl multiplicity (HW TRNG / ADC+Yarrow / getrandom / arc4random) places RNG in plugin tier.
Fallback validated by SCE c13_beta_antiflood tests (baseline ‚ą™ plugin lookup, 4570a389).
Counter-offer tracked as OQ-W24 (RFC ¬ß5.I tier-explicit separation, boundary hardening).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-6-h.intent: HMAC target plugin (OQ-W15 (a) Q2 ratified Round 11: per-SoC accelerator selection; sce_hmac_sha256 consumed by listener links with untrusted_source).
caveats_bullets: Q2 = Yes ratified Round 11: proposal target_plugin path confirmed by SCE maintainer.
Per-SoC accelerator surface (STM32H7 CRYP / ESP32 SHA / Cortex-M0+ SW) keeps baseline crypto-free.
SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind; wire output identical across backings.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-7-h.intent: HW semaphores/mailboxes target plugin (OQ-W14) ‚ÄĒ sce_hw_sem_*/sce_hw_mbox_*; required at multi-core MCU bring-up (Phase C cross-core); single-core MVP omits.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-sym.intent: Symbol surface partition into 7 categories (atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem) marked core or target-plugin; OQ-W15/W14 gate unresolved cells.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/3-whi.intent: Whitelist policy 2x2 matrix ‚ÄĒ core if every platform has body AND choice is not deploy-meaningful; target plugin if author/vendor needs to pick between non-interchangeable bodies.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/4-dep.intent: deploy.yaml extern_symbols block (RFC ¬ß5.K) names runtime crate variant + target plugin; activates 3-tuple per RFC ¬ß5.J.3; three concrete deploy states (mcu_target/ap_standalone/ap_mcu_pair).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-1-t.intent: StatechartHal trait (Rust pseudocode) ‚ÄĒ now_us monotonic tick microseconds, wake cooperative-scheduler hint, irq_save/restore (Phase C+ critical section).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-2-m.intent: HAL method body mapping per platform ‚ÄĒ now_us via DWT/SysTick on MCU vs Instant::now on AP linux; wake via worker_wake or tokio reactor; irq_save/restore via ¬ß2.4 or empty.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-3-t.intent: HAL trait belongs in sce_intrinsics_runtime (statechart concern), not sce_link_runtime_* (I/O concern); both consumers share one symbol surface as single dependency.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-no-.intent: RFC ¬ß5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait ‚ÄĒ provided by sce_intrinsics_runtime, bottoms out on ¬ß2 symbols; trait shape MCU-class per RFC ¬ß5.J.4.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/6-sel.intent: Self-review against ARCHITECTURE ¬ß2.4 six invariants ‚ÄĒ every row PASS; symbol surface identical across platforms with body-elided per platform-class (same shape, different body).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/7-nex.intent: Next-step scaffolding when Phase A SCE codegen lands ‚ÄĒ sce_intrinsics_runtime_c skeleton, _rust skeleton, target_extensions_stm32h747.yaml; OQ-W23 deferral noted.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/8-cha.intent: Change log ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-01 initial draft (third of three runtime-crate sibling docs; symbol surface partitioned into 7 categories; OQ-W15 (a) initial proposal locked in).

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Two binary ratification decisions on sce_intrinsics_runtime_* whitelist membership for RNG and HMAC primitives, individually decidable but coupled by shared consumer.
inputs_bullets: Shared consumer: stateless_accept: cookie_hmac_sha256 (RFC ¬ß5.K hard error if absent)
Decisions are individually decidable ‚ÄĒ RNG location does not constrain HMAC location
outputs_bullets: Decision 1: sce_random_fill(buf, len) -> int ‚Üí core whitelist (yes/no)
Decision 2: sce_hmac_sha256(key, key_len, msg, msg_len, out) ‚Üí target plugin (yes/no)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Phase A entry of public-Internet-facing MCU listeners is blocked until both stateless_accept primitives have ratified placement.
rationale_bullets: RFC ¬ß5.K stateless_accept required when domain_attrs.untrusted_source: true (build refusal)
stateless_accept body uses sce_hmac_sha256 (cookie HMAC) and sce_random_fill (key generation)
Both symbols must be placed (whitelist or plugin) before SCXML can author stateless_accept
inputs_bullets: Exposure class: domain_attrs.untrusted_source flag on listener link
deploy/stateless-accept-required-on-untrusted-source ‚ÄĒ hard error if missing
outputs_bullets: Public-Internet-facing MCU listener Phase A blocked pending placement decision
Private-LAN MCU listener Phase A not gated (caps alone suffice)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Watching-zenoh proposes RNG to core whitelist (universal entropy primitive) and HMAC to target plugin (per-SoC accelerator selection).
rationale_bullets: RNG universal ‚ÄĒ every platform has some entropy source (HW RNG / ADC noise / OS getrandom)
RNG no SoC-specific accelerator selection ‚ÄĒ bundled with HAL, single canonical symbol
RNG WCET cleanly handled by canonical symbol with measured bound, no per-deploy redeclaration
HMAC HW-accelerator selection genuinely per-SoC (CRYP/SHA/SW fallback)
HMAC SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind (option 3 fallback path)
HMAC bounded blast radius ‚ÄĒ keeps whitelist small, contains crypto precedent
inputs_bullets: Decision questions from ¬ß1 of this doc (RNG location / HMAC location)

alternatives_rejected.reason: loses universality, every public-Internet deploy ships plugin
not viable, entropy generation is platform-coupled
adds 32-byte crypto, sets precedent for more crypto primitives
loses HW accelerator, ~10x CPU on STM32H7, tight WCET headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Detailed counter-option matrix for the two decisions, expanding the trade-offs that ¬ß3 cites in summary form.

alternatives_rejected.reason: every deploy ships entropy plugin even when platform is canonical
not viable; entropy is irreducibly platform-coupled
HW dispatch survives but precedent for more crypto additions set
zero plugin dep but HW unused, ~10x CPU, tighter burst headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Forward-compatibility implications across the 2x2 matrix of RNG and HMAC placement decisions.
rationale_bullets: RNG core (proposal): RNG features land free; missing entropy ‚Üí build-time refusal
HMAC plugin (proposal): future crypto land by precedent; whitelist stays small
HMAC plugin (proposal): SoC-vendor crypto IP changes absorbed without RFC churn
RNG plugin alt: duplicated authoring per canonical deploy; each new RNG re-states it
HMAC core alt: faster Phase A on listener MCUs; precedent for crypto whitelist adds

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Ratification ask: yes/no on each of the two proposal decisions, with optional counter-direction and audit artifact in the OQ-W15 entry.
inputs_bullets: Initial proposal (this doc) as ratification subject
Counter-option matrix as alternatives if proposal rejected
outputs_bullets: Ratify or reject proposal (yes/no on each of two decisions)
If reject: counter-direction from matrix or proposed independently
Decision artifact: short note in rfc-open-questions-log.md OQ-W15 entry
caveats_bullets: OQ-W15 (b) MCU/AP defaults are out of scope here; settle post-Phase-A via HIL measurement
Decision Outcome (Round 11, 2026-05-14): Q1 = No, Q2 = Yes ratified by SCE maintainer.
Q1 rationale: BASELINE_SYMBOLS = architecture-fixed primitives only; RNG ‚Üí plugin tier.
Q2 rationale: per-SoC accelerator selection genuinely plugin-meaningful (proposal aligned).
Counter-offer (tier separation) tracked as OQ-W24; spec restructure deferred to next round.
Fallback path validated (SCE C13-ő≤ tests, 4570a389); deploys unblock via 4-line plugin row.

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Citation index linking this artifact to its source material in the watching-zenoh internal docs.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/1-framing-o.intent: Reassembly FSM between codec (Fragment) and network FSMs; chain key=(peer_zid, reliability) ‚ÄĒ two buffers per peer per upstream wire shape.
inputs_bullets: Chain key (peer_zid 16B, reliability 1bit); no priority, no sn_base in key
Wire: Fragment {sn:u64 VLE, payload:ZSlice, more bit, reliability bit}
FRAGMENT_FIRST/DROP exts patch-1+; legacy patch-0 infers first via empty+consecutive SN
Fragment classification: First / Continue / Final / Drop / unmatched-continue dropped at Router

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/10-next-ste.intent: Reassembly FSM unblocks Phase A SCXML authoring of sources/network/reassembly.scxml; deploy.yaml reassembly_pool block fully named; OQ-W22 closed.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-1-hierarc.intent: ReassemblyDispatcher = Router region + N parallel Slot regions (N=slot_count); one-active-per-key invariant; codegen N is build-time const.
inputs_bullets: Router: chain-key‚Üíslot-index map (bounded-collection<SlotEntry, N>), per-peer quota check
Slot[0..N-1]: parallel sub-region, four-state machine each
Upstream divergence: shared bounded pool decoupled from peer count (vs upstream inline 2√ópeers)
Parity invariant: slot_count ‚Č• 2 √ó peer_table.capacity reproduces upstream no-exhaustion
MCU slot_count=4, AP=32 (OQ-W8); over-commits trade strict parity for memory cost

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-2-slot-st.intent: Slot four-state FSM: Empty ‚Üí Receiving ‚Üí Complete / TimedOut / Aborted (three terminals funnel back to Empty via slot release).
inputs_bullets: Empty: slot free, awaiting allocation from Router
Receiving: chain in progress; consecutive Continue allowed
Complete: Final received with consecutive SN
TimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
Aborted: 7 reasons (OOO/Drop/duplicate-First/oldest-wins/codec-error/unmatched/peer-drop)
Slot release transitions buffer-pool slot cpu-mut/cpu-ref ‚Üí free (lifecycle FSM funnel)

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-3-router-.intent: Router region routes Fragment.* events to Slot regions by chain key; enforces per-peer quota; refuses second First on already-open key (duplicate-first abort).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-transit.intent: Eight detailed transitions across Slot states: Empty‚ÜíReceiving (First), Receiving‚ÜíReceiving (Continue), Final‚ÜíComplete, plus 5 abort variants and Terminal‚ÜíEmpty release funnel.
outputs_bullets: ¬ß2.4.1 Empty‚ÜíReceiving: Fragment.First admitted by Router (key not-active, quota OK, slot OK)
¬ß2.4.2 Receiving‚ÜíReceiving: Fragment.Continue with consecutive SN appends payload
¬ß2.4.3 Receiving‚ÜíComplete: Fragment.Final consecutive SN, emit message-complete event
¬ß2.4.4 Receiving‚ÜíAborted: out-of-order Continue (non-consecutive SN, strict in-order)
¬ß2.4.5 Receiving‚ÜíTimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
¬ß2.4.6 Receiving‚ÜíAborted: oldest-wins eviction by Router under pool pressure
¬ß2.4.7 Receiving‚ÜíAborted: codec error on payload decode (¬ß2.4.7a peer-drop via DROP ext)
¬ß2.4.8 Terminal‚ÜíEmpty: slot release funnel; lifecycle FSM cpu-mut‚Üífree

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-5-out-of-.intent: Strict in-order policy (OQ-W21 closed): non-consecutive Continue aborts the chain (option 2 ratified); upstream consecutive-SN parity preserved.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-6-mapping.intent: Mapping prose four-state Slot FSM (Empty/Receiving/Complete/TimedOut/Aborted) to RFC ¬ß5.M three-state high-level sketch (Idle/Active/Done) with terminal split.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/3-tx-fragme.intent: TX fragmentation sibling FSM ‚ÄĒ chunks outbound NetworkMessage exceeding link MTU into Fragment.{First, Continue, Final} with consecutive SN.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/4-timer-quo.intent: Reassembly timer / per-peer quota / pool config from deploy.yaml ‚ÄĒ reassembly_timeout_ms, per_peer_quota, slot_count, slot_size live under buffer_pools.reassembly_pool.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/5-trust-cla.intent: Trust-class interaction ‚ÄĒ reassembly forbidden on untrusted/session_arming links; established_session sibling instance receives reassembly bindings (OQ-W22 close).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/6-buffer-po.intent: Buffer-pool lifecycle FSM (¬ß5.E) interaction ‚ÄĒ reassembly slot acquire = pool slot cpu-mut; terminal release = cpu-mut/cpu-ref ‚Üí free.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-1-reassem.intent: Build-time reassembly capacity check ‚ÄĒ max_fragments √ó per_peer_quota √ó peer_table.capacity sufficiency for slot_count; hard error if violated.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-2-stage-c.intent: Build-time stage-copy rate warning ‚ÄĒ expected_p99_bytes / mtu_bytes ratio against pool slot reuse policy; warning if ‚Č•25%.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-3-reassem.intent: Build-time reassembly slot_size recommendation (informational) ‚ÄĒ slot_size ‚Č• max_fragments √ó mtu_bytes for full chain absorption.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-4-stage-c.intent: Build-time stage-copy WCET hard error ‚ÄĒ expected_p99_bytes √ó memcpy_cycles / clock_freq ‚Č§ worker_slot_budget_us; rejects M0+ deploys with oversized stage-copy.

C11 header shape for sce_link_runtime_lwip ‚ÄĒ opaque sce_link_t, sce_link_event_t enum, RX dispatch entry, sce_pool_slot_handle_t for ¬ß5.E ownership edge.
deploy.yaml links.<name>.driver mapping ‚ÄĒ four values (lwip_udp/lwip_tcp/serial_uart/raw_eth) for bare_metal; raw_eth via target plugin (vendor MAC HAL).
Trust-class compile-time elision ‚ÄĒ three classes (untrusted/session_arming/established_session); absent methods not declared (compile fail, not runtime); listener two-instance emission per OQ-W22.
Self-review against ARCHITECTURE ¬ß2.4 six invariants ‚ÄĒ every row PASS; static handles, bounded queues, header platform-gated to (c11, bare_metal).
Next-step when Phase A C11 emitter lands ‚ÄĒ sce_link_runtime_lwip crate skeleton, per-deploy .h generation, target_plugin driver impls; direct authoring blocker for Phase A entry.
Change log ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-01 initial draft (KICKOFF #2); header shape locked four outbound + 6 events + poll + ISR; trust-class compile-time elision; typestate via Clang -Wconsumed.

reassembly-fsm sec 6 ‚ÄĒ pool ownership FSM edges (free ‚Üí cpu-mut ‚Üí dma-armed ‚Üí cpu-ref ‚Üí free)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-1-position-.intent: Scouting is pre-session ‚ÄĒ turns abstract "join Zenoh mesh" intent into concrete unicast endpoint feeding session FSM Init‚ÜíLinkOpening.
inputs_bullets: deploy.scouting.{mode, timeout_ms, hello_max_peers}
links.udp_scout (trust_class=untrusted)
connect=/listen= deploy fields (used by static to bypass scouting)
Local node zid (16B ZenohID) and whatami (peer/client) compile-time consts
outputs_bullets: scout.hello.received(zid, whatami, locators[]) ‚ÄĒ feeds session FSM outbound Init‚ÜíLinkOpening
scout.timeout ‚ÄĒ terminal 'no peers found within scouting.timeout_ms'
scout.hello.peer_table_full ‚ÄĒ diagnostic, HelloPeerTable hit hello_max_peers cap

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-2-scout-hel.intent: Scout (version/what/zid) and Hello (version/whatami/zid/locators[]) wire messages on UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446.
inputs_bullets: Scout: version u8 (0x09) / what u8 bitmask (router=1, peer=2, client=4, default 3) / zid 16B
Hello: version u8 / whatami u8 (single value) / zid 16B / locators len-prefixed array
Default UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446; SCOUT_BUFFER_SIZE=32B; default timeout 1000ms
scout_rx_pool.slot_size 256B (>> Scout encoded ~19B upper bound)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-3-scouting-.intent: Scouting link is separate link instance from session links even on shared UDP multicast group/port; trust_class differs (untrusted vs session_arming).

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-4-three-mod.intent: Three modes ‚ÄĒ active (zenoh-pico parity Included) / passive (Phase D+ deferred via OQ-W23 close) / static (config bypass parity).
inputs_bullets: active: _z_scout_inner one-shot (exit_on_first true|false) ‚ÄĒ zenoh-pico parity
passive: no zenoh-pico equivalent ‚ÄĒ deferred Phase D+, OQ-W23 closed
static: scouting bypass via connect=/listen= deploy fields ‚ÄĒ parity, scouting FSM never instantiated
MVP mode enum = {active, static}; passive lands at Phase D customer-driven need

alternatives_rejected.reason: weakens zenoh-pico parity contract (no upstream daemon)
reversibility asymmetry, removing harder than adding (forward-namespace-0)
schema simpler, but loses uniform mode enum

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/10-next-step-.intent: Scouting FSM unblocks Phase A SCXML authoring (active + static modes); declare_fsm authoring contract listed; passive deferred Phase D+.
outputs_bullets: Phase A SCXML authoring of sources/session/scouting.scxml for {active, static}
declare_fsm.scxml authoring contract documented (Mech1+Mech2 transport-class guard)
deploy.yaml scouting block stable (mode/timeout_ms/hello_max_peers)
Passive mode body + 3 deploy fields preserved in OQ-W23 entry for Phase D+ revival

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-1-hierarchy.intent: ScoutingDispatcher composes ModeSelector + Active body region (instantiated iff mode‚ąą{active,passive}) + HelloPeerTable; codegen elides regions deploy-mode doesn't require.
inputs_bullets: ModeSelector: deploy.scouting.mode read at codegen time, compile-time const not runtime branch
Active region: states Idle/Sending/AwaitingHello/Cooldown (only when mode‚ąą{active,passive})
HelloPeerTable: bounded-collection<HelloEntry, hello_max_peers>, keyed by zid, per-dispatcher
static mode emits near-empty stub; matches Z_FEATURE_SCOUTING compile-time gate discipline

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-2-states-un.intent: Five states union across modes: Idle / Sending / AwaitingHello / Cooldown (passive) / Bypassed (static); per-mode usage is a subset.
inputs_bullets: Idle: waiting for session-open trigger (active) or next period (passive)
Sending: encoding + transmitting one Scout frame on scouting link
AwaitingHello: polling RX path until scouting.timeout_ms; loops if exit_on_first=false
Cooldown: passive-only inter-period dwell; cooldown_timer = scout_retry_interval - timeout
Bypassed: static-only marker; emits synthetic scout.hello.received from deploy connect[] at startup

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-3-transitio.intent: Active mode transitions Idle‚ÜíSending‚ÜíAwaitingHello with hello.received guards (capacity/dedup); Cooldown for passive; Bypassed terminal for static synthesizes events.
inputs_bullets: Idle‚ÜíSending on session_open.requested (active) or passive_period.elapsed (passive)
Sending: encode+link.send; immediate‚ÜíAwaitingHello; tx_failed‚ÜíIdle (diag scout/tx-failed)
AwaitingHello: scout_timer; hello.received guards (capacity, zid-dedup, exit_on_first)
Cooldown (passive only): cooldown_timer = scout_retry_interval_ms - timeout_ms
Bypassed (static): synth scout.hello.received per deploy.connect[] entry at startup; terminal

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-1-active.intent: Active body lifts zenoh-pico __z_scout_loop (scout.c:29-140) into states; per-line mapping to Sending entry / AwaitingHello self-loop / hello.received.
inputs_bullets: scout.c:57 link_send_wbuf ‚Üí Sending entry action
scout.c:60-62 RX setup + clock ‚Üí AwaitingHello entry, arm scout_timer
scout.c:63 elapsed loop ‚Üí AwaitingHello self-loop until scout_timer.elapsed
scout.c:73-78 message decode ‚Üí codec kind on scouting link, decode fail = drop+diag
scout.c:121-123 exit_on_first ‚Üí guard on AwaitingHello‚ÜíIdle transition
MVP active = implicit-scout (one Hello, take it); scouting.exhaustive=true deferred

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-2-passive.intent: Passive deferred Phase D+ (OQ-W23 closed 2026-05-01); MVP enum={active,static}; deploy/scouting-mode-unknown rejects passive value pre-Phase-D.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-3-static.intent: Static = scouting-absent; one synthetic scout.hello.received per deploy.connect[] entry at startup; saves ~1.5KB SRAM via codegen elision on MCU.
inputs_bullets: deploy.connect[] locator list verbatim; matches zenoh-pico _z_locators_by_config
Codegen elides scouting link, scout codec, scout buffer pool (~1.5KB SRAM saved on MCU)
udp_session link still authored; only scouting setup omitted under static
Trust-class clarity: locators trusted-by-construction (deploy file) vs untrusted (wire)
Distinct mode value gives distinct diagnostics on session-open failure

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-5-timeouts-.intent: Two timers ‚ÄĒ scouting.timeout_ms (1000ms zenoh-pico parity) + link.scouting.open_timeout_ms (0ms always-on); passive timers deferred Phase D+.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-6-bounded-c.intent: HelloPeerTable cap = hello_max_peers; two invariants ‚ÄĒ cap ‚Č§ peer_table+multicast cap (hard error), slot_size ‚Č• Hello upper bound (informational).
inputs_bullets: Invariant 1 (hard error): hello_max_peers ‚Č§ peer_table.capacity + multicast_peer_table.capacity
Verification: MCU 8 ‚Č§ 16+8=24 ‚úď; AP 64 ‚Č§ 256+64=320 ‚úď
Invariant 2 (informational): scout_rx_pool.slot_size ‚Č• HelloUpperBoundBytes(hello_max_peers)
Diagnostic scouting/hello-max-peers-exceeds-peer-tables (hard error)
Diagnostic scouting/hello-slot-size-recommendation (informational)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-2-oq-w3-ans.intent: OQ-W3 closed: Interest is not router-only in 1.x ‚ÄĒ three transport-class mechanisms (unicast acceptor push / multicast Interest pull / client-router push).
outputs_bullets: Mechanism 1 (unicast peer-peer): acceptor pushes ALL local declares + DeclareFinal at handshake
Mechanism 2 (multicast peer mesh): joining peer Interest{CURRENT,KEYEXPRS} ‚Üí each remote replies
Mechanism 1 (client unicast to router): router-side same as peer-peer
Client unicast no router: not supported (Mech1 absent, Mech2 absent, inbound Interest = no-op)
MCU multicast peer must implement both directions; unicast-only MCU does only Mech1 acceptor push

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-3-implicati.intent: Scouting FSM doesn't participate in Interest itself, but mode choice determines which Interest mechanism downstream session-establishment fires.
outputs_bullets: mode=active ‚Üí unicast session-open ‚Üí Mechanism 1 (acceptor push at handshake)
mode=passive ‚Üí multicast peer join ‚Üí Mechanism 2 (Interest pull + multicast reply)
mode=static + connect[] ‚Üí Mechanism 1 (same as active for resulting unicast handshake)
Scouting is the trigger chain; declare_fsm authors consult here for Interest-trigger boundary

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/6-1-per-link-.intent: Trust-class table extension: untrusted required on scouting links / forbidden elsewhere; three-layer mechanical defense (wire / session-fsm / reassembly).
inputs_bullets: untrusted: required on scouting / forbidden on session+reassembly
session_arming: forbidden on scouting / required on listener / forbidden on reassembly
established_session: forbidden on scouting / required on data-plane / required on reassembly
Layer 1 (wire): trust_class deploy field validated by RFC ¬ß5.K
Layer 2 (session-fsm): no Accepting.* on untrusted (session-fsm ¬ß2.6 row 1)
Layer 3 (reassembly): forbids reassembly pool binding on untrusted (reassembly-fsm ¬ß5)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/6-4-oq-w9-clo.intent: OQ-W9 closed: zenoh-pico clients refuse multicast sessions (UNSUPPORTED) but participate in multicast scouting; codegen rejects whatami=client + multicast session link.
inputs_bullets: whatami=peer + multicast scouting + multicast session: OK (_z_multicast_open_peer)
whatami=client + multicast scouting: OK (scouting layer is whatami-agnostic)
whatami=client + multicast session link: REFUSED (_Z_ERR_CONFIG_UNSUPPORTED_CLIENT_MULTICAST)
Build diag: deploy/client-multicast-session-unsupported (hard error)
session-fsm ¬ß3.4 amend OQ-W9 status open‚Üíanswered, see scouting-fsm ¬ß6.4

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/8-1-authoring.intent: Three gaps (G-SCT-1 resolved via OQ-W23 defer; G-SCT-2 unsolicited Hello broadcaster speculative; G-SCT-3 Hello slot-size worst-case sizing).
inputs_bullets: G-SCT-1 resolved: passive mode deferred Phase D+ (OQ-W23 close, MVP={active,static})
G-SCT-2 speculative: unsolicited Hello broadcaster (no OQ filed; await deploy scenario)
G-SCT-3 informational: scout_rx_pool slot_size vs Hello worst-case (multi-homed AP, 8 locators)
Recommendation: scouting/hello-slot-size-recommendation diagnostic (informational, not blocker)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/8-2-new-open-.intent: OQ-W23 / OQ-W3 / OQ-W9 ‚ÄĒ all three closed in this document; cross-ref pointers added to rfc-open-questions-log entries.
inputs_bullets: OQ-W23 answered/deferred: passive mode Phase D+; (b) defaults preserved in OQ entry
OQ-W3 closed in ¬ß3.2: not router-only, three transport-class mechanisms documented
OQ-W9 closed in ¬ß6.4: client refuses multicast session, participates in scouting

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/10-next-.intent: Document unblocks deploy.yaml skeletons + runtime crate API stubs + scouting/reassembly FSM sketches; does NOT unblock SCXML authoring (waits for SCE Phase A).
outputs_bullets: deploy/*.yaml skeleton authorable ‚ÄĒ timer / lease / handshake / closing fully named
Runtime crate API stubs ‚ÄĒ link event contract no longer hand-waved
scouting-fsm.md sibling sketch (active/passive/static)
reassembly-fsm.md sibling sketch (RxDispatch + Fragment row inputs)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-1-unic.intent: Unicast session FSM has eight states plus Closed terminal; splits Opening (initiator) and Accepting (acceptor) explicitly.
inputs_bullets: Init: created, link not yet open; entry from outbound or inbound
LinkOpening: outbound link driver attempting open (TCP connect / WS upgrade)
Scouting: active scout loop pre-target (mode=active deploys only)
Opening: initiator driving 4-way Init/Open handshake
Accepting: acceptor responding to remote-driven handshake
Established: handshake done, RX/TX regions enabled (sub-region container)
Closing: graceful close in progress; sends Close with reason
Closed: terminal, link released, pool slots freed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-2-unic.intent: Unicast transitions ‚ÄĒ outbound (LinkOpening ‚Üí Opening.SentInitSyn ‚Üí GotInitAck ‚Üí Established) + inbound (Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn ‚Üí SentInitAck ‚Üí SentOpenAck ‚Üí Established).
outputs_bullets: Outbound: Init ‚Üí LinkOpening ‚Üí Opening.SentInitSyn ‚Üí GotInitAck ‚Üí Established
Inbound: Init ‚Üí Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn ‚Üí SentInitAck ‚Üí SentOpenAck ‚Üí Established
Inbound entry guarded by half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token (silent drop if denied)
Cookie validation guard: Accepting.SentInitAck ‚Üí SentOpenAck rejects invalid/expired cookie
stateless_accept mode: AwaitingInitSyn‚ÜíSentInitAck does not allocate FSM instance (cookie-only)
Timeouts: init_ack/open_ack/init_syn/open_syn ‚Üí Closing(GENERIC) or Closed(inbound-stall)
Framing errors ‚Üí Closing(reason=INVALID); peer Close ‚Üí Closed(peer-rejected)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-3-esta.intent: Established is parallel SCXML region with 4 concurrent sub-machines: RxDispatch / TxSchedule / Keepalive / LeaseMonitor.
inputs_bullets: RxDispatch: Frame‚ÜíNetworkMessage / Fragment‚Üíreassembly / KeepAlive‚Üíreset / Close‚Üíparent / OAM‚Üídiag
TxSchedule: Idle / Batching / Flushing per (reliability, priority) band
Keepalive: Armed / Sending; timer = lease √ó keepalive_ratio (default 1/3)
LeaseMonitor: Healthy / Warning / Expired; warning at lease√ó2/3

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-4-clos.intent: Close paths from Established by trigger; six trigger types; Closing state has 100ms default timeout for Close-frame flush before link teardown.
outputs_bullets: session.close() ‚Üí Closing(GENERIC) ‚Üí Closed
LeaseMonitor.Expired ‚Üí Closing(EXPIRED) ‚Üí Closed
RxDispatch peer Close ‚Üí Closed (no reciprocal Close)
Framing error ‚Üí Closing(INVALID|UNSUPPORTED) ‚Üí Closed
link.lost ‚Üí Closed (no Close frame)
TX congestion budget exhaust on RELIABLE batch ‚Üí Closing(UNRESPONSIVE) ‚Üí Closed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-5-time.intent: All session-FSM timers named with single deploy.yaml home; 12 timers covering link / handshake / scout / lease / keepalive / closing / batch / reassembly / accept-rate / cookie.
inputs_bullets: link.open_timeout (5s) / init_ack_timeout / open_ack_timeout (2s) / scout_timeout (1s)
lease (10s, negotiated) / keepalive_interval (lease/3) / closing_timeout (100ms)
batch_timer (1ms AP / 5ms MCU) / reassembly_timeout (500ms per peer-priority-reliability-sn_base)
accept_rate_window (1s) / accepting_inactivity_timeout (1s)
cookie_hmac_lifetime (30s) / cookie_hmac_key_rotation (1h, old key honored one extra lifetime)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-6-trus.intent: Trust-class on each link gates hardening rules; session_arming requires quota + accept_rate; established_session is sibling for post-handshake traffic.
inputs_bullets: untrusted: Scout/Hello-only links, never spawns Accepting
session_arming: hosts Accepting cluster; quota + accept_rate required (hard error)
established_session: post-handshake Frame/Fragment, no Accepting attachment
untrusted_source=true on session_arming ‚Üí stateless_accept required (not just recommended)
Listener with session_arming emits sibling established_session instance (OQ-W22 resolution)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-7-acce.intent: Accept-side hardening ‚ÄĒ three caps in series: half-open capacity / per-source token bucket / optional stateless_accept HMAC cookie.
inputs_bullets: Half-open capacity: session_arming_quota bounds concurrent Accepting.* instances per listener
Per-source token bucket: accept_rate_per_sec / accept_rate_burst per (link, src_addr)
Stateless accept (optional): cookie_hmac_sha256 defers FSM allocation until cookie validated
Three caps chain in series; silent drop on cap denial (no Close frame, anti-amplification)
deploy.yaml emits all three as bounded-collection checks + token-bucket guard at Init‚ÜíAccepting

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-1-mult.intent: Multicast session-level FSM: Idle ‚Üí LinkOpening ‚Üí Running (parallel: JoinEmit / RxDispatch / PeerSweep) ‚Üí Stopped.
inputs_bullets: JoinEmit: periodic Join (default 2500ms upstream interval); state Idle/Sending
RxDispatch: classify Join/Frame/Fragment/KeepAlive/Close/OAM on TransportMessageLowLatency
PeerSweep: timer lease/3, evict PeerTable entries with last_seen > lease
Idle‚ÜíLinkOpening on create(multicast_link); LinkOpening‚ÜíRunning on link.ready

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-2-mult.intent: Per-peer FSM (one per zid in multicast_peer_table bounded-collection): Discovered ‚Üí Active ‚Üí Expired.
inputs_bullets: Discovered: first Join validated (version/resolution/batch/qos); init RX seq table
Active: any msg refresh last_seen; Close{zid} or lease expiry ‚Üí Expired
Expired: emit peer-lost event; PeerTable removes at next sweep
Rejection: version/max_sessions/SN-res/QoS mismatch ‚Üí ignored + diag (not a transition)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-3-no-c.intent: Multicast has no Closing state ‚ÄĒ peers time out via lease monitors; symmetric to upstream which has no multicast/close.rs.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-4-clie.intent: Client whatami refuses multicast session (zenoh-pico parity); participates in scouting only. OQ-W9 closed via deploy/client-multicast-session-unsupported.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/4-scouti.intent: Scouting is a sibling SCXML; produces Hello events that drive Init‚ÜíLinkOpening‚ÜíOpening. Three modes: active / passive / static.
inputs_bullets: active: Listening ‚Üí Sending ‚Üí WaitHello ‚Üí Connect; clients + peer initial bring-up
passive: Listening only; peers after mesh stable, receives unsolicited Hello
static: no scouting; deploy.yaml endpoints, session.create(outbound,endpoint) at boot
Scouting link distinct from session links even on shared UDP multicast group/port (different framer)
Hello from already-established peer = no-op (matched by zid; idempotent with session creation)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/5-3-q13-.intent: Q13 closed: two SCXML files split by transport class (unicast / multicast), NOT by mode (peer / client) ‚ÄĒ peer/client wire-identical at session layer.
outputs_bullets: sources/session/session_unicast.scxml ‚ÄĒ handles both peer and client unicast
sources/session/session_multicast.scxml ‚ÄĒ instantiated only on machines with multicast session link
whatami read from deploy at compile time, embedded in InitSyn, not branched on
Earlier session_peer.scxml + session_client.scxml framing retracted across ARCH ¬ß5 / RFC ¬ß7 C8
Parametric across unicast/multicast rejected ‚ÄĒ handshake vs no-handshake structurally different

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/6-link-l.intent: Link layer event contract ‚ÄĒ 6 inbound link events to FSM, 3 invocations from FSM to link; FSM never touches bytes (codec sibling kinds do).
inputs_bullets: link.ready: TCP/WS open ‚Üí LinkOpening‚ÜíOpening.SentInitSyn or attach Accepting
link.lost(cause): underlying link broken ‚Üí Closed(diag: link-lost)
link.rx(bytes): pass to codec, codec emits recv(MsgType) events
link.tx_drained: TX pool slot freed ‚Üí wake TxSchedule
link.backpressure(on|off): TX queue watermark crossed ‚Üí TxSchedule pause/resume
link.framing_error: codec decode failed ‚Üí Closing(INVALID)
outputs_bullets: link.open(): initiate connect (outbound only)
link.send(bytes, reliability): ship encoded TransportMessage
link.close(): tear down byte channel after Close flushed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/7-extens.intent: Extension negotiation ‚ÄĒ eight extensions read+written in same handshake transition as carrier message; ext::Auth may span multi-step within Opening.
inputs_bullets: ext::QoS / ext::QoSLink ‚ÄĒ InitSyn/InitAck; QoS Close(UNSUPPORTED) on incompat
ext::Auth ‚ÄĒ multi-step in InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck; OQ-W2/W10
ext::MultiLink ‚ÄĒ graceful downgrade to single-link on negotiation fail
ext::LowLatency ‚ÄĒ session refused on link-class mismatch
ext::Shm ‚ÄĒ always advertised absent in MVP; Close(UNSUPPORTED) on peer-critical
ext::Compression ‚ÄĒ accept-and-ignore (deploy extensions_ignored); OQ-W4 if peer-critical

watching-zenoh/what-it-does.title: What it does
watching-zenoh/what-it-does.intent: Two builds at once: an MVP wire-compatible subset of zenoh plus a single-source six-backend codegen pipeline.
rationale_bullets: zenoh-pico 1.5.x + zenoh 1.5.x wire interop for MVP subset, optional surfaces deferred to Phase B+
Same SCXML sources generate six backends via SCE Forge
Design SSoT is ARCHITECTURE.md; 11 spec docs governed by Mnemosyne

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp.intent: Enumeration of Zenoh 1.x wire-protocol types into MVP/Deferred/Permanent classes mapped to RFC sec 5 SCE kinds.
inputs_bullets: ARCHITECTURE sec 2 ‚ÄĒ MVP criterion (zenoh-pico parity) and Included/Deferred/Permanent split
rfc-sce-protocol-synthesis sec 5 ‚ÄĒ SCE Forge kinds backing each message category
Upstream zenoh-protocol crate enums and structs (one type = one classification unit)
outputs_bullets: Per-message classification (MVP/Deferred/Permanent) with responsible RFC sec 5 SCE kind
Cross-reference matrix sec 9.2 from message types to RFC sec 5 kinds
caveats_bullets: Status: Draft (2026-04-24); field-level shape refined when codec authoring begins (Phase B).
Unit of classification = one upstream zenoh-protocol type, not feature; preserves crate legibility.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/0-n.intent: Normative reference table ‚ÄĒ zenoh-protocol 1.5.0 (struct/enum source), VERSION 0x09, zenoh-pico parity target, upstream test vectors + pcap corpus.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/1-p.intent: 5-layer stack ‚ÄĒ Scouting/Transport/Network/Payload/Extensions; every byte belongs to exactly one layer; per-layer SCE kind unambiguous.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/10-.intent: Open questions OQ-W1..W8 ‚ÄĒ zenoh-pico release pin, Auth baseline, Interest no-router, ext::Compression critical bit, OAM drop semantics, batch defaults, PatchType, bounded defaults.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/11-.intent: Self-review checklist against ARCHITECTURE ¬ß2.4 six invariants ‚ÄĒ every row checkable true; failing during Phase B is design bug, file as RFC OQ or doc revision.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/12-.intent: Change log ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-24 initial draft (zenoh-protocol 1.5.0 / VERSION 0x09 grounded); OQ-W1..W8 filed; classification totals to re-verify against zenoh-pico.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/2-c.intent: Three-bucket classification legend (Included/Deferred/Permanent) aligned with ARCHITECTURE ¬ß2.1/2.2/2.3; Backing-kind column points at RFC ¬ß5 source.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/3-s.intent: Scouting layer ‚ÄĒ Scout/Hello messages (codec RFC ¬ß5.B); link kind with framer datagram; UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446 by convention.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-1.intent: Transport control messages ‚ÄĒ InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck/Close/KeepAlive (4-way handshake + lease); all Included; codec + statechart.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-2.intent: Data-carrying framing ‚ÄĒ Frame (priority/SN), Fragment (RFC ¬ß5.M reassembly), Join (multicast), OAM (decode-only); chain key (peer_zid, reliability) 2-tuple.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-3.intent: TransportMessageLowLatency variant for UDP unicast/multicast ‚ÄĒ union of Close/KeepAlive/NetworkMessage; selected statically per-link in deploy.yaml.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-4.intent: Transport-layer extensions on Init/Open/Join ‚ÄĒ QoS/QoSLink/Auth/MultiLink/LowLatency/PatchType Included; Shm/Compression deferred or permanent on MCU.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-t.intent: Transport (session) layer ‚ÄĒ per-peer session state authored as session_unicast/multicast.scxml; split by transport class; peer/client share unicast FSM (Q13).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-1.intent: Declare sub-variants ‚ÄĒ KeyExpr/Subscriber/Queryable/Token (declare+undeclare) + DeclareFinal; all Included; bounded-collection (RFC ¬ß5.L) backed.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-2.intent: Permanent-on-MCU network features ‚ÄĒ wildcard Interest aggregation across N peers, forwarding-table maintenance, global key-space indexing; deferred on AP.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-n.intent: Network (routing) layer carried inside Frame/Fragment ‚ÄĒ Push/Request/Response/ResponseFinal/Interest/Declare/OAM; Interest bounded form; aggregation router-only.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-1.intent: PushBody variants ‚ÄĒ Put (publish value with timestamp/encoding/sinfo/attachment/payload) and Del (delete key); both Included; codec-backed.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-2.intent: RequestBody variant ‚ÄĒ Query (parameters/consolidation/sinfo/body/attachment); Included; codec + statechart query FSM.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-3.intent: ResponseBody variants ‚ÄĒ Reply (timestamp/encoding/sinfo/payload) and Err (encoding/sinfo/payload, reply-channel error); Included; codec + statechart.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-z.intent: Zenoh payload layer ‚ÄĒ innermost user payload carried inside Push/Request/Response; PushBody/RequestBody/ResponseBody variants enumerated in ¬ß6.1-¬ß6.3.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-1.intent: Common app-visible extensions ‚ÄĒ Timestamp/Attachment/NodeId/SourceInfo/Consolidation/Encoding/QoS Included; SHM permanent on MCU / deferred on AP.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-2.intent: Unknown extension policy ‚ÄĒ ignorable bit set: decode envelope discard body; critical bit unset: framing error ‚Üí Close{unsupported_extension}.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-e.intent: Extension chain TLV with more-bit per RFC ¬ß5.B; per-extension budgets in deploy.yaml; decode-time enforcement with diagnostic on overflow.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/8-t.intent: Transport/link matrix ‚ÄĒ message-class √ó link-class table (TCP/UDP-uni/UDP-multi/Serial/WS); per-link framer in deploy.yaml; new classes via target plugin.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-1.intent: Per-layer Included/Deferred/Permanent counts ‚ÄĒ Scouting 2/0/0, Transport 10/0/0, Network 16/0/3, Payload 5/0/0, Extensions 7/1/0.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-2.intent: RFC ¬ß5 kind cross-reference table ‚ÄĒ ¬ß5.A/B/C/D/E/F/I/J/K/L/M/N each used by; every MVP message covered by ‚Č•1 kind; gaps file as RFC OQ.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-s.intent: Scope classification summary ‚ÄĒ message-type counts per layer + Cross-reference to RFC ¬ß5 kinds; every Included row covered by ‚Č•1 kind.

changes_bullets: sources/algorithms/whatami_v1_valid.scxml new ‚ÄĒ ¬ß3 Hello + ¬ß4.1 Init/Join cbyte whatami 2-bit field validator. Allowlist {0=router, 1=peer, 2=client}; rejects wire value 3 (would decode to API enum 0x08 outside constants.h:50-54 surface).

sources/algorithms/transport_ext_id_v1_valid.scxml new ‚ÄĒ ¬ß4.4 per-control-message extension FULL_ID allowlist. 2-param (t_mid, ext_header) ‚Üí bool. T_INIT allows {0x27 INIT_PATCH}; T_OPEN allows {} (skip-all per transport.c:328); T_JOIN allows {0x27 JOIN_PATCH, 0x51 JOIN_QOS}. Fragment (¬ß4.2) extensions out of scope (extension_v1_dispatch R19 covers).

Round 40.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking skeleton lands ‚ÄĒ `crates/` Cargo workspace + `wz-codecs` crate that hosts sce-codegen Rust output for the ¬ß6 payload trio (msg_put + msg_del + 5 deps). First piece of running code in the project ‚ÄĒ cargo build passes, smoke test exercises encode/decode round-trip on default instances. 38 rounds of spec authoring now have a Rust binary that compiles.
changes_bullets: crates/Cargo.toml new ‚ÄĒ wz Rust workspace root. resolver=2; members=[wz-codecs]. Workspace package shares license + edition + rust-version. [workspace.lints.rust] warnings = "deny" for production gate parity with SCE workspace.

crates/wz-codecs/ new (4 files): Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/smoke.rs. Cargo.toml declares deps on sce-forge-runtime (path) and sce-build (build-dep, path) into vendor/sce. lib.rs declares 8 `pub mod <stem> { include!() }` blocks at crate root for codegen-output sibling resolution.

crates/wz-codecs/build.rs invokes sce-build::compile_forge_with_imports() in-process at compile time, mirroring vendor/sce/sce-forge-runtime/rust/build.rs pattern. 8 SCXMLs compiled in dep-order: timestamp/encoding/ext_unit/ext_zint/ext_zbuf/ext_entry/msg_put/msg_del. cargo:rerun-if-changed wired on sources/codecs/.

SCE codegen template bug workaround in build.rs post-process: SCE emits `#![doc = "SCE-MAP: stem:line"]` as a file-leading inner attribute. When wz includes the file inside `pub mod X { include!(...); }`, rust rejects (E0658 / inner-attribute-not-permitted at item position). SCE's own `cargo test -p sce-forge-runtime --features alloc` hits the same wall ‚ÄĒ verified R40. Workaround strips the redundant inner-attr line; the SCE-MAP info itself is also emitted on the very next line as a regular `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` comment, so zero info loss. Proper fix is an SCE upstream PR (R41 carry ‚ÄĒ see below).

tests/smoke.rs (66 lines, 2 tests): msg_put + msg_del Default‚Üíencode‚Üídecode‚Üíassert-fields-match round-trip. Establishes the encode/decode self-consistency baseline that Layer 3 (wire-interop against zenoh-pico bytes) will graduate from in R41/R42.

.gitignore extended: `crates/target/` ignored (workspace build output). `crates/Cargo.lock` COMMITTED per Cargo's binary-producing-workspace recommendation for reproducible Layer 3 builds across CI/dev/prod.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: pass (Compiling wz-codecs v0.1.0, Finished 'dev' profile, 0 errors, 0 warnings).

cargo test -p wz-codecs: 2 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored. Both msg_put + msg_del default encode‚Üídecode round-trips assert all fields match (header byte, payload_len, payload bytes, present-if Optional gates all None).

sce-codegen output (in OUT_DIR): 8 .rs files (timestamp.rs, encoding.rs, ext_unit.rs, ext_zint.rs, ext_zbuf.rs, ext_entry.rs, msg_put.rs, msg_del.rs). All `use super::X::Y;` cross-module references resolve through wz_codecs::lib's `pub mod X { include!() }` declarations.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 39‚Üí40, T3 warn 856 unchanged.
First running Rust code in the project. Prior 38 rounds were spec authoring (12 atomic docs, 36 SCXML, vendored SCE, mnemosyne adoption); R40 is the FIRST round whose deliverable executes.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR carry ‚ÄĒ `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2` change template to emit SCE-MAP info as `/// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (outer doc, attaches to first struct) or just `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (regular comment) instead of `#![doc = ...]` (inner attribute). Inner attribute on a file consumed via `include!(...)` inside `pub mod X` is rejected by rust E0658 ‚ÄĒ broken in SCE's own `--features alloc --tests` build too, just not caught because default build doesn't trigger include!(). Single-line template change; would let wz-codecs/build.rs drop the post-process strip.

R41 = `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` ‚ÄĒ vendor zenoh-pico as git submodule (sibling to vendor/sce/), build via cc/cmake, generate FFI bindings via bindgen, expose `_z_put_encode` + `_z_keep_alive_encode` + `_z_init_encode` + sibling `_*_decode` Rust signatures. This is the foundation for Layer 3 wire-interop tests.

R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` ‚ÄĒ Layer 3 test crate. For each codec in wz-codecs, construct a logical message instance, encode via wz-codecs Rust path AND via zenoh-pico-sys FFI path, byte-compare. First REAL validation that the codec SCXMLs match zenoh-pico wire-format. This is the round that PROVES the 36 SCXML rounds correctly modeled the wire protocol; if it fails, the diagnosis loop is short because each codec is independently testable.
R43+ = expand wz-codecs to host all 21 codec SCXMLs (currently only 8 of 21 wired). Layer 3 coverage grows in lockstep.
R45+ (post-Phase 2) = runtime crates wz-runtime-tokio + wz-runtime-lwip per the design docs. Session FSM (R29 statechart) wiring becomes possible once runtime trait surface lands.

Round 41.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking-skeleton FFI bridge lands ‚ÄĒ `vendor/zenoh-pico` submodule + `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` cmake+bindgen crate. First FFI call from wz Rust into zenoh-pico C library succeeds (4/4 smoke tests on `_z_id_len`). Layer 3 wire-interop foundation in place; R42 can now construct byte-equivalence assertions between wz-emit and zenoh-pico-emit for the same logical message.
changes_bullets: vendor/zenoh-pico submodule added (eclipse-zenoh/zenoh-pico) pinned at 3b3ab65c (1.9.0+10 commits). Matches the R5 zenoh-pico reference state used throughout R29+ SCXML authoring evidence chain. .gitmodules updated.

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/ new crate (Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/ffi_smoke.rs). Build-deps: cmake 0.1 + bindgen 0.71. Crate-level `links = "zenohpico"` declares the static library to cargo's link DAG.

build.rs two-stage: (1) cmake::Config invokes vendor/zenoh-pico/CMakeLists.txt with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF + minimum-features flag set, produces libzenohpico.a under OUT_DIR/lib/; (2) bindgen parses include/zenoh-pico.h with allowlisted symbols only (`_z_id_t` + `_z_id_len` for R41 smoke). Linux/macOS platform macro (`ZENOH_LINUX` / `ZENOH_MACOS`) derived from cargo TARGET env var ‚ÄĒ zenoh-pico's platform.h errors out on missing macro at clang parse time.

src/lib.rs `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/bindings.rs"))` exposes the FFI surface with the bindgen-required `#![allow(non_*case*)]` set. Documented as raw FFI ‚ÄĒ NOT a safe wrapper. Layer 3 test harness (R42+) is the intended consumer.

tests/ffi_smoke.rs (4 tests): _z_id_len exercised across all four `_z_id_t.id[16]` topologies (all-zero / partial-trailing-zeros / all-non-zero / mid-buffer-trailing-nonzero). Each asserts the C function's strip-trailing-zeros loop behavior matches expected length, proving the FFI bridge transfers struct-by-value correctly across the C ABI boundary.
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended to include zenoh-pico-sys.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p zenoh-pico-sys: pass. CMake configures + builds + installs libzenohpico.a (with all upstream platform support layers compiled), bindgen emits bindings.rs into OUT_DIR, rustc links the static lib into the test binary. ~30s cold-build, sub-second warm.
cargo test -p zenoh-pico-sys: 4/4 passed / 0 failed. All four _z_id_len cases (lengths 0/5/16/10) match the expected output of zenoh-pico's strip-trailing-zeros loop at src/protocol/core.c:35-45.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 40‚Üí41, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

FFI integrity proven empirically: (a) bindgen-emitted `_z_id_t` struct layout matches C's `struct { uint8_t id[16] }` (4 different non-zero patterns transfer correctly); (b) static linker resolves _z_id_len from libzenohpico.a; (c) C function called by Rust returns correct uint8_t values across the FFI boundary.
carry_forward_bullets: R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` Layer 3 wire-interop tests. Expand zenoh-pico-sys allowlist to include `_z_keep_alive_encode` (simplest body, single MID + 0 body bytes ‚ÄĒ matches the keep_alive.scxml codec wz emit). Then expand to msg_put / msg_del / scout / hello / etc. Each new codec verified = one new allowlist entry + one new test. Per-codec round-trip: (a) construct a known logical message, (b) encode via wz_codecs::X::encode(), (c) encode via zenoh-pico's _z_*_encode FFI, (d) byte-compare. If they differ, the wz codec SCXML's spec mapping has a bug ‚ÄĒ first REAL spec validation.

Round 42.decision_summary: Layer 3 wire-interop verification lands. First objective evidence in 42 rounds that a watching-zenoh codec SCXML is byte-equivalent to zenoh-pico 1.9.0's wire encoding: close codec emits identical bytes to _z_close_encode across 10 reason-byte test cases. The spec is no longer just an internally-consistent artifact ‚ÄĒ it is wire-format-validated against the canonical reference implementation for the first codec.
changes_bullets: wz-codecs/build.rs CODECS array extended with close + frame + fragment. wz-codecs/src/lib.rs gains matching pub mod declarations. cargo build pass.

zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h new ‚ÄĒ bindgen umbrella that pulls in zenoh-pico.h PLUS internal protocol/iobuf.h + protocol/codec/transport.h + protocol/codec/message.h. Layer 3 needs the internal _z_*_encode surface which is intentionally not transitively included by the public umbrella.

zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist expanded with _z_t_msg_close_t + _z_wbuf_t + _z_zbuf_t + _z_close_encode + _z_wbuf_make + _z_wbuf_len + _z_wbuf_clear + _z_wbuf_to_zbuf + _z_zbuf_clear. Each entry justified by the close Layer 3 test.
crates/wz-integration-tests/ new crate ‚ÄĒ Phase 2 Layer 3 wire-interop test host. dev-deps wz-codecs + zenoh-pico-sys + sce-forge-runtime.
tests/layer3_close.rs (110 lines, 2 tests): byte-compare across 10 reason values + wire-shape pin (1-byte body invariant). FFI byte extraction via wbuf -> zbuf -> _ios._buf[.._w_pos].
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended (wz-integration-tests added).
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 8/8 pass / 0 fail. wz-codecs smoke 2/2, zenoh-pico-sys ffi_smoke 4/4, wz-integration-tests layer3_close 2/2.

Layer 3 close exercises the FULL FFI pipeline: bindgen-emitted _z_t_msg_close_t constructed in Rust; _z_close_encode called across C ABI; bytes extracted from _z_wbuf_t via _z_wbuf_to_zbuf + _z_iosli_t._buf[.._w_pos]; resources cleaned via _z_zbuf_clear + _z_wbuf_clear.

Byte-equivalence: Close { reason }.encode() (wz) == _z_close_encode(wbf, header, &_z_t_msg_close_t { _reason: reason }) bytes (zenoh-pico) for every reason in {0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06, 0x07, 0x42, 0xFF}. 10/10 match.

carry_forward_bullets: R43 = Layer 3 expansion to frame + fragment codecs. Both are ¬ß4.2 data-carrying with VLE sn + tail payload. Adds _z_frame_encode + _z_fragment_encode to zenoh-pico-sys allowlist. First VLE wire-byte agreement validation ‚ÄĒ VLE u64 is the most pervasive primitive across the codec set.
R44 = Layer 3 across handshake bodies: init_body + open_body + join + scout + hello. Exercises multi-bit flag, dotted-path length-ref, present-if local + parent.X, repeat, import primitives.
R45 = Layer 3 across payload bodies: msg_put + msg_del + ext_entry + child codecs (timestamp / encoding / ext_unit/zint/zbuf). Variant + embed + tlv-chain + entry-flag termination validated.

changes_bullets: zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist gains: _z_t_msg_frame_t + _z_t_msg_fragment_t + _z_slice_t + _z_delete_context_t + _z_frame_encode + _z_fragment_encode + _z_slice_as_zbuf. All justified by R43 Layer 3 tests.

tests/layer3_fragment.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): VLE width boundary corpus (sn=0/1/127/128/256/16383/16384/1M/u32::MAX) √ó payload variants (empty / 1-byte / multi-byte). Non-owning _z_slice_t wrap of Rust &[u8]; empty payload skipped via NULL start pointer + _z_slice_check short-circuit.

tests/layer3_frame.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): same VLE corpus via the frame path. Construction shape differs from fragment ‚ÄĒ _z_t_msg_frame_t._payload is *mut _z_zbuf_t (NOT _z_slice_t by value); zbuf built via _z_slice_as_zbuf wrapping a Rust slice, stored in MaybeUninit to avoid unused_assignments lint on the empty-payload null branch. Empty payload: _payload=null short-circuit per upstream code.

Cross-codec frame‚ÜĒfragment body-equivalence: when header=0 (no Z flag, no first/drop extensions), frame and fragment produce identical body bytes for the same (sn, payload) input. The transport-layer MID distinction (T_FRAME 0x05 vs T_FRAGMENT 0x06) is written by the envelope encoder, not the body codecs.

changes_bullets: .github/workflows/ci.yml new (~150 lines). Single ubuntu-22.04 runner job with: checkout (submodules: recursive) + apt cmake/libclang/clang/perl + rust toolchain (stable, with rustfmt+clippy) + cache (Swatinem/rust-cache v2 for crates + vendor/sce) + mnemosyne-cli install (graceful skip on failure) + build-sce.sh + mnemosyne validate-workspace + per-codec verify-codegen.sh sweep with audit-traced LE-divergence allowlist for init_body+join + cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.

wz-codecs/src/lib.rs gains crate-level `#![allow(clippy::all)]` ‚ÄĒ the entire crate body is sce-codegen include!()'d output, so clippy style lints are SCE template authoring concerns not consumer-tunable. Workspace rustc `warnings = "deny"` policy stays in force (rustc warnings ARE consumer-actionable).
zenoh-pico-sys/src/lib.rs gains `#![allow(clippy::all)]` for the same reason ‚ÄĒ entire crate is bindgen FFI output.

wz-integration-tests test files cleaned: `manual_range_contains` (3 places) ‚ÄĒ `zid_len >= 1 && zid_len <= 16` ‚Üí `(1..=16).contains(&zid_len)`; `unnecessary identity map` (msg_put: `(0..255u8).map(|i| i).collect()` ‚Üí `.collect()`); doc list indentation in fragment + init_body. All clippy lints in human-authored code resolved at source, not muted.
